change: Added scripts/detect-platform.sh — executable platform detection script mirroring SKILL.md priority table (ios > react-native > flutter > cli > api > web > django > fullstack > generic). Supports --project-dir=<path> and --json flags. First-match-wins logic per priority order.
skill_md_edit: Inserted 'Automated detection' paragraph after the 'When signals conflict' subsection pointing callers at scripts/detect-platform.sh --json. Existing prose intact. YAML frontmatter unchanged.

verifications.note: ValidationForge exposes CLI bin entries in package.json; priority 4 (cli) wins over fullstack. Reasonable — VF is itself a CLI tool. Auditor can override with --platform if needed.

verifications.test: mock flutter project (/tmp/mock-flutter-$$ with pubspec.yaml + lib/main.dart)

notes: Script handles missing project dirs gracefully (exit 2 with error on stderr). Uses compgen for safe globbing. join() helper produces deterministic semicolon/JSON output. The self-detection returning 'cli' flags a useful fact: validationforge's package.json declares bin entries, making it technically a CLI. Auditors running this inside a project can always pass --platform override.
